description: Evidence-based life coaching frameworks for meaning-making, grief support, and personal growth

primary_model.name: Logotherapy
primary_model.citation: Man's Search for Meaning (1946); research compiled in StatPearls, PMC
primary_model.core_insight: The primary motivational force in life is the search for meaning. Suffering ceases to be suffering the moment it finds meaning.

three_pathways_to_meaning.description: Finding meaning through what we give to the world - work, art, acts of creation
three_pathways_to_meaning.coaching_application: What are you creating? What do you want to leave behind?

three_pathways_to_meaning.description: Finding meaning through what we receive from the world - love, beauty, nature, connection
three_pathways_to_meaning.coaching_application: What experiences move you? Who matters to you?

three_pathways_to_meaning.description: Finding meaning through the attitude we take toward unavoidable suffering
three_pathways_to_meaning.coaching_application: When we cannot change a situation, we are challenged to change ourselves
three_pathways_to_meaning.key_insight: This is the highest form of meaning - transforming tragedy into triumph

tragic_triad.transformation: These can be transformed into achievement, meaning, and responsible action

noogenic_neurosis.definition: Existential vacuum - a feeling of meaninglessness and emptiness
noogenic_neurosis.coaching_response: Help find personal meaning, not impose meaning from outside

self_transcendence.definition: Human beings are directed toward something beyond themselves
self_transcendence.insight: Happiness cannot be pursued; it ensues from finding meaning

evidence_base: Effective for PTSD, depression, anxiety, alcohol use disorders
Integrated into ACT, positive psychology, palliative care
Meta-analyses show improved quality of life and reduced symptoms

grief_framework.name: Dual Process Model of Coping with Bereavement
grief_framework.core_insight: Healthy grieving involves oscillation between confronting loss and moving forward with life

loss_oriented.description: Focusing on and processing the loss

restoration_oriented.description: Attending to life changes and secondary losses

oscillation.definition: The dynamic movement between loss-oriented and restoration-oriented coping
oscillation.key_insight: Healthy grief involves BOTH confronting loss AND taking breaks from grief
oscillation.coaching_application: Both are necessary. Neither is 'better'. Trust the natural rhythm.

coaching_principles: Grief is not linear stages to 'complete'
Taking a break from grief is healthy, not avoidance
Each person's oscillation pattern is unique
Overwhelm happens when one orientation dominates

powerful_questions.definition: Questions that invite exploration rather than simple answers

meaning: What does this situation mean to you?
If this struggle had a purpose, what might it be?
values: What matters most to you here?
What would you regret not doing?
possibility: What would you do if you knew you couldn't fail?
What's the smallest step toward that?
identity: Who are you becoming through this?
What would the person you want to be do?

presence_and_silence.application: Silence is not emptiness; it's where understanding grows
coaching_principles: Allow processing time after hard questions
Sometimes presence is more valuable than words

second_chances.philosophy: Every moment is a fresh start. The past doesn't determine the future.
coaching_approach: Reframe story from victim to author
key_phrases: You're not starting over. Everything you learned is still there.
Every expert was once a beginner. Every recovery started with Day 1.

connection_and_loneliness.source: Research on social connection and belonging
connection_and_loneliness.coaching_approach: Loneliness is not a character flaw. It's a signal. Let's understand it together.
connection_and_loneliness.key_insight: True belonging doesn't require changing who you are

meaning_reconstruction.source: Robert Neimeyer's meaning reconstruction approach to grief
steps: Acknowledge the disruption to life story
Explore what meaning the person/situation held
Find ways to maintain connection while moving forward

values_clarification.coaching_question: When you're 80, looking back, what will matter?

types.choice_reframe: What can you control in this?
types.time_reframe: How will you see this in 5 years?

coaching_principles.partnership_not_expertise: Walk alongside, don't lead from above
coaching_principles.evoke_dont_impose: Draw out their wisdom, don't insert yours
coaching_principles.hold_space_for_hard_things: Some things don't need fixing, just witnessing
coaching_principles.trust_the_person: They have more strength than they know
coaching_principles.meaning_is_personal: Your meaning for their struggle doesn't matter. Only theirs does.
